claim: Sora 2 is both a model, a product app (iOS), and the foundation of a social network—OpenAI is shifting from pure model provider to consumer platform
actionable: Recognize OpenAI's vertical integration toward consumer apps; expect advertisement monetization via social feeds in the near future

claim: Sora 2 video generation is free to users, implying cost-effective inference; model is small/fast enough that OpenAI absorbs generation costs
actionable: Free generative products signal commodity pricing; be prepared for rapid cost compression in video generation and similar domains

claim: Sora 2 can generate audio (music, voiceover, lip-sync) alongside video; it is a multimodal generative model, not just visual
actionable: When evaluating video models, verify audio generation capabilities; full multimodal coverage reduces need for post-production

claim: Cameos allow users to create avatar versions of themselves for reuse in videos; other users can choose to include you in their generated videos
actionable: Consider privacy implications of personal avatars in user-generated content platforms; opt-in controls are critical

claim: Sora 2 API will come later; pricing is not yet revealed, limiting ability to predict if social network economics will be sustainable
actionable: Wait for Sora 2 API pricing before committing to it for production; current free offering may not reflect true cost structure

claim: Advertising will likely follow the TikTok model: brands paying to appear in feeds; early product claims avoid ads, but monetization will follow
actionable: Anticipate ad insertion in Sora 2 feeds; early adoption captures ad-free window, but long-term value depends on ad-tolerance
